commit.message:
Fit all windows within 600 px height for small Haiku screens

- Reduced ExtractionWindow tips scroll area: 130→70 min, 180→90 max
- Reduced RoastColorWindow thumbnail: 320×240 → 320×180
- Reduced RoastColorWindow tips scroll: 130→70 min, 200→100 max
- Removed unnecessary 40px height buffer from all three windows' ResizeTo() calls
- Updated BRIEF.md to reflect thumbnail size changes and window optimization

All tool windows now fit within 600px total height (content + title bar + borders)
to accommodate smaller Haiku displays.

commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

commit.message:
feat: search relevance, skeleton mode, graph limits, trace_data_flow (#6)

* docs: update README for new tools and defaults

- Add search_imports to MCP tools table
- Update map_overview description with tiered detail levels
- Update read_source description with truncation behavior
- Update search_symbols description with multi-word support
- Update default_token_budget from 4000 to 32000
- Update tool count from 12 to 13

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix cargo fmt formatting issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve clippy warnings for CI

- Use div_ceil() instead of manual division
- Derive Default for Config instead of manual impl
- Rename Direction::from_str to parse to avoid trait confusion
- Add Default impl for DepGraph
- Use or_default() instead of or_insert_with(Vec::new)
- Merge identical if-else branches in python/java visibility
- Use next_back() instead of last() on DoubleEndedIterator
- Use &Path instead of &PathBuf in function signatures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove unused debug/warn imports after merge with main

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: improve search relevance for short and multi-word queries

- Short query terms (<=2 chars like "ai") now use prefix/exact matching
  instead of substring LIKE, preventing "ai" from matching "trait", "email"
- search_symbols now also matches file paths, so symbols in relevant
  directories rank higher
- Ranking improved: name match (-10) > path match (-5) > signature (-3)
- Variables/consts deprioritized in rankings (usually noise)
- compress_context TF-IDF: short terms require exact token match, not
  substring, reducing false positives in scoring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add read_source skeleton mode and get_ranked_symbols path filter

- read_source mode='skeleton' returns signatures only (~100 tokens)
  instead of full source (97K+ chars for large components like ChatPage)
- get_ranked_symbols path='backend-service/src/' scopes PageRank results
  to a specific directory, enabling targeted architecture analysis

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update README with skeleton mode, path filter, search improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply short-term exact matching to search_imports

Short query terms (<=2 chars) in search_imports now match against
source_module exactly or as path component (prefix/suffix), not as
substring. "ai" now finds `from 'ai'` and `react-icons/ai` but not
tailwind-merge, react-toastify, email, etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add output limits to get_dependencies and get_impact

- get_dependencies: sort by PageRank, add max_results (default 20),
  show "N total, showing top M" summary
- get_impact: add max_depth (default 3) and max_results (default 30),
  group output by file, show summary count ("N symbols across M files")
- Add DepGraph::get_impact_bounded() for depth-limited BFS
- Add DepGraph::file_of() for symbol-to-file lookup

Fixes issue where respondStream returned 500+ deps and PugDisplayData
blast radius was 113K chars. Now both give compact, ranked output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add trace_data_flow tool for cross-file flow tracing

New MCP tool that follows a symbol upstream (callers) and downstream
(callees) across files, showing the call chain with file paths and
symbol kinds. Supports max_depth and max_results limits.

Example: trace_data_flow("respondStream") shows:
  - Upstream: 254 callers across backend/frontend
  - Downstream: 1200 callees (logger, prisma, config, etc.)

Also updates README: 13 → 14 tools, adds trace_data_flow to table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix message view stuck on Loading: move expandedQuotes before load()

Show only primary category services + GBP category cards on home page

The home page was showing all services with primary-category URL patterns,
causing 404s for services belonging to secondary categories. Now only
primary category services are listed, with secondary GBP categories shown
as clickable cards linking to their category pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Google OAuth profile display: show full name and avatar (#5)

Google OAuth provides the user's full name in user_metadata.name but RootLayout was only checking display_name, causing the profile to fall back to the email. This resulted in incorrect initials (e.g. "WG" for willzhang058@gmail.com). Now properly extracts full_name or name from Google's metadata. Also displays the Google profile picture instead of initials when available, and makes the fallback initials match the navbar styling.

commit.message:
Add Main CPU ↔ Sub CPU cross-reference tables to inter-cpu-protocol

Documents the complete data flow from Main CPU sender routines to Sub CPU
handlers: command range mapping (8 handlers), special commands (E1/E2/E3),
boot-time transfers, runtime audio pipeline (8 stages), MIDI voice
dispatch, and naming convention correspondences between both CPUs.

commit.message:
fix: camera follows mesh position directly, eliminating player/camera jitter

Camera now tracks localMesh.position instead of raw server position array.
Mesh uses velocity extrapolation + lerp-40 between 64TPS server updates,
camera follows the smooth mesh = no flitting between positions.

commit.message:
Add edit completed tracks feature (#506)

## Summary

- Add ability to edit completed karaoke tracks: cleans up existing
outputs (YouTube, Dropbox, GDrive, GCS), recycles brand code, resets
state to AWAITING_REVIEW
- Support optional artist/title metadata updates during edit (triggers
screens regeneration)
- New `POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/edit` endpoint with cleanup tracking and
review URL response
- Edit button in OutputLinks for completed jobs with confirmation modal
explaining implications

## Changes

### Backend
- `backend/api/routes/jobs.py` - New edit endpoint with full output
cleanup (YouTube, Dropbox, GDrive, GCS finals), brand code recycling,
state transition, and optional metadata update
- `backend/models/job.py` - Added COMPLETE → AWAITING_REVIEW state
transition and `edit_count` field
- `backend/tests/test_edit_completed_track.py` - 14 unit tests covering
success, metadata updates, validation, and cleanup

### Frontend
- `frontend/components/job/EditTrackModal.tsx` - Confirmation modal with
optional artist/title editing
- `frontend/components/job/OutputLinks.tsx` - Edit button (amber) for
completed jobs with outputs
- `frontend/lib/api.ts` - `editCompletedTrack()` API method and
`EditTrackResponse` type
- `frontend/components/__tests__/OutputLinks.test.tsx` - 3 new tests for
Edit button visibility

### Docs
- `docs/API.md` - Documented new endpoint
- `docs/README.md` - Added recent changes entry
- `docs/archive/2026-03-06-edit-completed-tracks-plan.md` -
Implementation plan

## Test plan

- [x] 14 backend unit tests for edit endpoint (success, metadata,
validation, cleanup)
- [x] 3 frontend tests for Edit button visibility (complete job, deleted
outputs, non-complete)
- [x] All 576 frontend unit tests passing
- [ ] Manual: Edit a completed track, verify outputs are cleaned up and
review reopens
- [ ] Manual: Edit with metadata change, verify screens regenerate

<!-- @coderabbitai ignore -->

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: SDRAM 位扩展（16-bit → 32-bit，双颗粒）

- sdram.v：新增 CHIP_SEL 参数，DPI-C 函数名按颗粒区分（sdram_lo/sdram_hi）
- sdram_top_apb.v：实例化 2 个颗粒（u_sdram_lo/u_sdram_hi），dq 16→32，dqm 2→4
- sdram_axi_core.v：SDRAM_DATA_W=32，SDRAM_DQM_W=4，MODE_REG BL=1，去掉 STATE_WRITE1，
  读路径去掉 data_buffer_q，ACK 改为 rd_q[SDRAM_READ_LATENCY]
- ysyxSoCFull.v：APBSDRAM 端口/顶层端口/内部 wire/颗粒实例化全部更新
- test_bench_soc.cpp：lo/hi 两个独立 map，sdram_lo/hi_read/write 四个 DPI-C 函数

  mag/accel/gyro calibration; add save_calibration() (DCD save cmd 0x06);

commit.message:
feat(navigate): realistic medieval cartography with painted terrain regions

Complete rewrite of map rendering for maximum realism:

Parchment background:
- Multi-layer noise textures (fine grain + coarse paper fiber)
- Radial vignette with darkened edges
- Foxing spots, crease lines, water stains (aged paper look)

Terrain illustrations (medieval caterpillar style):
- Mountains: side-view ridges with cross-hatching shadow lines, snow caps
- Forests: overlapping layered canopy clusters (back-to-front depth)
- Swamps: oval water areas with horizontal wave lines + reed stalks
- Deserts: rolling dune ridges with stippled wind marks + sand dots
- Volcanic: dark rock cones with lava cracks and smoke wisps
- Rocky terrain: boulders with crack details and light spots
- Grassland: multi-blade tufts with wildflower dots

Painted biome regions (WoW-style):
- Soft colored blobs under terrain art for zone identity
- Gaussian blur filter for painted-area feel
- Opacity scales with exploration (discovered vs known)

Enhanced decorations:
- Illuminated manuscript border with spiral curl flourishes
- Triple-line frame (outer thick, middle thin, inner dotted)
- 32-tick compass rose with depth-shaded cardinal needles
- Sea serpent with fin detail and tail curl
- Label underline flourishes on explored locations

Split into 3 files for maintainability:
- navigate-map.js: orchestration, defs, parchment, aging, pan/zoom
- navigate-map-terrain.js: biome regions + detailed illustrations
- navigate-map-decor.js: border, roads, markers, compass, decorations

commit.message:
feat: expand cooperative capabilities — groups, state, tasks, voting, election

Protocol v0.3.0 with 30+ new message types for multi-agent coordination:

- Capabilities: announce/query peer skills, tools, model
- Groups/channels: join/leave named groups, group messaging
- Shared state: distributed key-value store (global or group-scoped)
- Presence: rich status updates (idle/busy/working + task description)
- Task broadcast: fan-out tasks to all/filtered peers, collect responses
- Task delegation: assign tasks to specific peers, track status, get results
- Consensus/voting: propose votes, collect choices, tally outcomes
- Leader election: Bully algorithm with auto-re-election on leader disconnect
- Delivery confirmation: ACK/NACK/QUEUED for message delivery tracking
- Request-response correlation: req_id matching (no more sleep hacks)
- Rate limiting: token bucket per connection on the server
- Message queuing: offline peers get messages when they reconnect
- Auto-reconnect: exponential backoff with session identity preservation
- Keepalive: configurable client-side ping loop with dead connection detection
- Auth: optional shared token authentication on the server

26 MCP tools (up from 7). 156 tests (up from 55). All passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Wave 8 — weekly report card, before/after timeline, menu optimizer, share snapshot, review suggestion, competitor alert (§247–§252)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: equal-power crossfade at interval boundaries (#118)

Replaces linear fade-in with equal-power (sin²+cos²=1) crossfading for all
interval transitions, not just peer joins. Captures last 256 interleaved samples
(128 per channel, matching NINJAM's MAX_FADE) from each peer's outgoing interval
and blends into the incoming interval head using sin/cos weights. Preserves energy
throughout the transition, eliminating perceived loudness dips.

Also adds sine_roundtrip_across_intervals test to characterize audio quality
through slicing and reassembly.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
